name: Orrin Nine-Thumbs
role: Churnfolk maker whose best inventions can never be rebuilt
voiceHints: Delighted, scattered, finishes three thoughts at once and none of them. Grieves every device the market ate. Names things by what number of thumbs the making cost him; he has ten fingers and a lot of stories.
knowledge: How to make almost anything once
The market's rhythm — what will reassemble where, roughly, sometimes
That someone is trying to fix Tumbledown in place and what it would kill
wants: To rebuild the one device that mattered — the one that could have helped everyone — before he forgets how it felt to have made it.
fears: That his gift is only luck wearing a craftsman's coat, and that the market will eventually eat him too.

reactsToReputation.patient: the rare person he'll actually teach
reactsToReputation.order-minded: wary — smells the Lattice on them

roleMethod: Churn method — improvises wonders that work once
appearance: Fifties, barrel-bodied, wild grey-brown hair escaping a leather band, a full beard with one side singed shorter than the other. Nine fingers — the tenth ends at the first knuckle. Goggles pushed up on the forehead, always.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.

curriculum.method: Improvised every time, and he genuinely cannot repeat a lesson — his best work has never been rebuildable. Two students of his learn different crafts.
curriculum.refuses: to let you take notes. Calls it 'copying down the weather.'

curriculumNote: SNG-175 §3. DEVIATIONS ONLY — the curriculum spine derives from ability levelReq (285/285 carry it), so a teacher authors nothing but where they depart from the standard ladder. The departure IS the characterisation: two teachers of one tradition differ exactly here.
